_comment: Apps that MUST NEVER be flagged as unused-and-removable, even if UserAssist + Prefetch show zero launches in 90+ days. These are background/utility/dependency apps whose value is not measured by user-launch counts. The unused-apps module reads this list before proposing any uninstall — anything matching gets filtered out silently (or logged as 'kept: dependency/security/etc.'). Match on display name substring (case-insensitive) against ARP (Add/Remove Programs) entries.
_matchRule: Substring match against DisplayName in HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\* + HKLM:\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\* + HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\*. Match on Publisher too where noted.

security_av.reason: Security tools intentionally run silently. Zero user launches is normal, not a signal to remove.

cloud_sync.reason: Sync clients run headless. User rarely opens the UI — but the daemon is doing its job.

vpn.reason: VPN clients are typically launched via tray/toggle or run always-on. Low UserAssist activity is meaningless.

password_manager.reason: Password managers are used via browser extension or system-tray — the desktop app itself may see zero launches.

backup.reason: Backup software runs on schedule. Zero UI launches is expected.

cloud_dev_cli.reason: CLI tools launched via terminal — do not appear in UserAssist (Explorer-only surface).

runtime_framework.reason: Frameworks and runtimes have no UI — apps depend on them. Removing breaks things silently.

system_essentials.reason: OS or driver components that end up in ARP but are essential.
